Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 1:

James, thanks for taking on this cleanup.

This patch is too big to land before we branch b2_8, since it will almost certainly continue to conflict with any changes to LNet and we are down to only critical/blocker fixes at this point, and minor improvements to test scripts and such.

Could this be split into smaller independent chunks by file, maybe 5-6 parts of 500-600 LOC?  That would allow some parts to land even if there are conflicts with other parts.

Patch Set 1:

I do plan on breaking this patch up unless Oleg rather have a single drop during the freeze phase. From what I see 3 potential patches for lnet are set for landing to 2.8. Currently I'm working on this patch to make it a base patch to test real code changes from upstream to ensure nothing breaks.

Patch Set 7:

Currently I like to get this patch in shape for landing but I like to delay until the patch for LU-7101 lands. The reason is ORNL is interested in the LU-7101 being back ported to 2.8. Once this patch lands back porting to 2.8 will be more challenging. Still the feedback is most useful. Especially since any improvements here can be feed back upstream. Thanks Andreas.

Patch Set 9:

From the feedback to appears people feel that its better to keep upstream a separate tree from master so this work will be dropped.

Abandoned

From the feedback to appears people feel that its better to keep upstream a separate tree from master so this work will be dropped.

Patch Set 9:

I don't have any objection to merging most of these changes into the master tree, and I didn't see any -1 reviews on this patch.

My main concern with the patch was that it is too large and contains too many different types of changes to inspect quickly, which also means that it gains patch conflicts very quickly.  Having smaller patches would be easier to inspect & land.

I'm definitely in favour of proper comment blocks, tab cleanups, remove spaces between function and (.  Not against non-splitting of strings.

Patch Set 9:

Hammond did make the point that the style cleanups would cause us to lose the git blame info about earlier patches. Are stand alone style patches worth it in that case?

Hi Andreas,

For the question from James, could you please advise?
Restored

parents.subject: LU-6142 lnet: remove all white spaces from LNet layer

commit.subject: LU-6142 lnet: non functionality cleanups for lnet
commit.message:
LU-6142 lnet: non functionality cleanups for lnet

This work brings the core LNet code into alignment
with what is required for the upstream kernel.
The changes are proper comment blocks, tab cleanup,
not splitting strings for debug reporting, space
between function name and open parenthesis. No
functionality has been changed in this patch.

Change-Id: Id6f3b1b366c2e582189d9af0a27aa97a4bcfc5dd
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
